title: Bridge Essay: What does the interactive brain hypothesis mean for social neuroscience? A dialo

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mind as Computer Critique** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** 4E cognition — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended mind; critique of computationalism.

**What does the interactive brain hypothesis mean for social neuroscience? Adialogue.**. Philos Trans R Soc Lond B Biol Sci.. PMID: 27069056. DOI: 10.1098/rstb.2015.0379. Arecent framework inspired by phenomenological philosophy, dynamical systems theory, embodied cognition and robotics has proposed the interactive brain hypothesis (IBH). Whereas mainstream social neuroscience views social cognition as arising solely from events in the brain, the IBH argues that social cognition requires, in addition, causal relations between the brain and the social environment.
